seoTitle: Markwood vs Marlo — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Markwood and Marlo. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Markwood and Marlo.
heading: Markwood vs Marlo — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Markwood, Victoria and Marlo, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Marlo has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Markwood (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Markwood or Marlo safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Markwood has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Marlo scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Markwood scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Marlo scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Markwood or Marlo?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Marlo has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
